_note: Concise, community-style playstyle notes per civ (archetype + how they tend to play + a signature tactic to watch for). General guidance for the DE 1v1 ladder, not exhaustive; corrections welcome.

Armenians.playstyle: Infantry and naval civ. Strong infantry backed by the healing Warrior Priest; flexible into archers.
Armenians.watch_for: Warrior Priest healing makes their infantry trades sticky.

Aztecs.playstyle: Infantry and monk civ with no cavalry. Leans on Eagles, monks and siege; great eco from extra carry capacity.
Aztecs.watch_for: Strong Men-at-Arms openings and powerful relic/monk play.

Bengalis.playstyle: Elephant, cavalry and naval civ. Archers into Battle Elephants and Ratha; resists conversion.
Bengalis.watch_for: Massed Battle Elephants in the late game.

Berbers.playstyle: Cavalry and naval civ. Cheap, fast stable units; Camel Archers and Genitours.

Bohemians.playstyle: Gunpowder and monk civ. Early Hand Cannons, Hussite Wagons and Houfnice; strong sci/eco techs.

Britons.playstyle: Foot-archer civ. The longest-range archers; cheaper Town Centers to boom into massed Crossbows/Longbows.
Britons.watch_for: Range advantage; do not melee into them, use Skirmishers + Mangonels.

Bulgarians.playstyle: Infantry and cavalry civ. Cheap Blacksmith/Militia upgrades and Konniks that survive death as infantry.
Bulgarians.watch_for: Strong Militia-line and Konnik timing pushes.

Burgundians.playstyle: Cavalry and gunpowder civ. Early economy upgrades and Castle-age Cavaliers; burst-charge Coustillier.
Burgundians.watch_for: Coustillier charge burst, and late-game Flemish Revolution turning villagers into infantry.

Burmese.playstyle: Infantry, monk and elephant civ. Battle Elephants, Arambai and free lumber upgrades.
Burmese.watch_for: Arambai deal huge but inaccurate damage; clump punishes you.

Byzantines.playstyle: Defensive civ. Cheap counter units (Skirmishers, Spears, Camels) and Cataphracts; great at fast Imperial.
Byzantines.watch_for: They out-counter you cheaply; Cataphracts shrug off anti-cavalry bonus.

Celts.playstyle: Infantry and siege civ. Fast infantry (Woad Raiders) and the strongest siege; Furor Celtica.
Celts.watch_for: Siege Rams/Onagers; their siege fires faster and has more HP.

Chinese.playstyle: Archer and gunpowder civ. Extra starting villagers and tech discounts; Chu Ko Nu.
Chinese.watch_for: Tricky strong start that snowballs into archers.

Cumans.playstyle: Cavalry civ. Can build a 2nd Town Center in Feudal (boom) and access Feudal siege; Kipchaks and Steppe Lancers.
Cumans.watch_for: Feudal Siege Workshop / Battering Ram all-in, or a greedy 2-TC boom.

Dravidians.playstyle: Infantry and naval civ. Urumi Swordsmen and strong fishing economy; Thirisadai on water.
Dravidians.watch_for: Urumi charge attack bursts your clumped units.

Ethiopians.playstyle: Archer and siege civ. Archers fire faster, free gold from aging up; Torsion Engines.

Franks.playstyle: Cavalry civ. Free farm upgrades, +HP cavalry and cheap Castles; the textbook Knight civ.
Franks.watch_for: Knight floods and forward Castle drops.

Georgians.playstyle: Defensive and cavalry civ. Monaspa that grow stronger in groups; bonuses on hills and when garrisoned.

Goths.playstyle: Infantry civ. Cheap, fast-trained infantry with +population; the classic late-game flood.
Goths.watch_for: Huskarls (great vs archers) and an endless cheap-infantry flood after Imperial.

Gurjaras.playstyle: Cavalry and camel civ. Shrivamsha that dodge projectiles, Chakram Throwers, mill-garrison eco.
Gurjaras.watch_for: Projectile-dodging cavalry; archers struggle into them.

Hindustanis.playstyle: Camel and gunpowder civ. Strong camels (Imperial Camel), Ghulam and Hand Cannons; cheaper villagers over time.
Hindustanis.watch_for: Camel-heavy armies and Imperial Camel Riders.

Huns.playstyle: Cavalry civ. No houses needed (fast start), strong Cavalry Archers (Tarkans) and cheaper Trebuchets.
Huns.watch_for: Very fast Feudal/Castle pressure thanks to the no-house start.

Incas.playstyle: Infantry and defensive civ. Kamayuks, Eagles and Slingers with cheap eco/blacksmith upgrades.
Incas.watch_for: Eagle + Kamayuk pushes and strong walls.

Italians.playstyle: Archer and naval civ. Cheaper age-ups, Genoese Crossbows, Condottieri and gunpowder; strong on water.
Italians.watch_for: Fast age-ups into Genoese Crossbows (anti-cavalry archers).

Japanese.playstyle: Infantry and naval civ. Fast-attacking infantry (Samurai) and strong fishing eco.
Japanese.watch_for: Strong Men-at-Arms openings; Samurai shred unique units.

Jurchens.playstyle: Cavalry and gunpowder civ. Tanky Iron Pagoda cavalry and area-damage Grenadiers.

Khitans.playstyle: Infantry and cavalry civ. Pasture economy, Liao Dao, Mounted Trebuchet and early Heavy Cavalry Archers.
Khitans.watch_for: Fast Heavy Cavalry Archer access in Castle Age.

Khmer.playstyle: Infantry, elephant and siege civ. Buildings need no prerequisites; Ballista Elephants and strong Scorpions.
Khmer.watch_for: Scorpion + elephant pushes; villagers can garrison in houses.

Koreans.playstyle: Defensive and naval civ. Free tower upgrades, War Wagons and Turtle Ships.
Koreans.watch_for: Tower rushes on land and a very strong navy on water.

Lithuanians.playstyle: Cavalry and monk civ. Relics give Knights/Leitis +attack; fast start with bonus food.

Magyars.playstyle: Cavalry civ. Cheap Scouts, free melee attack upgrades and Magyar Huszars.
Magyars.watch_for: Cheap scout openings flowing into knights or cavalry archers.

Malay.playstyle: Infantry, naval and elephant civ. The fastest age-up times and cheap Battle Elephants.
Malay.watch_for: Surprisingly early Feudal/Castle timings; cheap elephant spam.

Malians.playstyle: Infantry and cavalry civ. +pierce-armor infantry, Farimba cavalry and Gbeto.
Malians.watch_for: Tanky infantry that resists archer fire.

Mapuche.playstyle: Cavalry and counter-unit civ. Bolas Riders that snare, and Kona vs injured targets.
Mapuche.watch_for: Snaring ranged cavalry that pins your army.

Maya.playstyle: Archer civ. Resources last longer (great eco), Plumed Archers and Eagles; El Dorado Eagles.
Maya.watch_for: Relentless archer eco and Eagle + archer pushes.

Mongols.playstyle: Cavalry-archer civ. Faster hunters (fast start), Mangudai, quick Cavalry Archers and strong siege.
Mongols.watch_for: Hunt-fueled fast Feudal, and Mangudai + siege in Castle/Imperial.

Muisca.playstyle: Archer and monk civ. Guecha Warriors, Temple Guards and a monastery-based economy.
Muisca.watch_for: Skirmisher-type Guecha that heal on death.

Persians.playstyle: Cavalry civ. Faster, higher-HP Town Center and Dock; War Elephants and Savar in the late game.
Persians.watch_for: The TC-drop cheese (deleting and rebuilding the TC next to yours) and strong late cavalry/elephants.

Poles.playstyle: Cavalry civ. Folwark economy, stone-to-gold Knights, armor-shredding Obuch and Winged Hussars.

Portuguese.playstyle: Naval and gunpowder civ. Cheaper gold units, Organ Guns, Carracks and late-game Feitorias.
Portuguese.watch_for: Organ Gun + Treb + Monk compositions and Feitoria sustain.

Romans.playstyle: Infantry and cavalry civ. Legionaries and Centurions, cheaper infantry and strong Scorpions.

Saracens.playstyle: Camel and naval civ. Mamelukes, a powerful Market, and archers that hit buildings hard.

Shu.playstyle: Archer and siege civ. White Feather Guard that snare, and the swappable War Chariot.

Sicilians.playstyle: Infantry and cavalry civ. Donjons with Serjeants, First Crusade, and reduced bonus damage taken.
Sicilians.watch_for: They take reduced bonus damage, so your counters work less well; Donjon drops.

Slavs.playstyle: Infantry and siege civ. Cheap siege, Boyars and a strong farming economy.
Slavs.watch_for: Infantry into knights/siege with a fat farm eco.

Spanish.playstyle: Gunpowder and monk civ. Conquistadors, Missionaries, faster building and gold-free Blacksmith/Univ upgrades.

Tatars.playstyle: Cavalry-archer civ. Keshiks that generate gold, Flaming Camels and hill/los bonuses.
Tatars.watch_for: Sustained Cavalry Archers funded by Keshik gold.

Teutons.playstyle: Infantry and defensive civ. Teutonic Knights, tanky towers/TC, cheap farms and longer monk range.
Teutons.watch_for: Slow but extremely tanky pushes; towers and Teutonic Knights.

Tupi.playstyle: Cavalry and infantry civ. Blackwood Archers and Ibirapema Warriors trained in pairs.

Turks.playstyle: Gunpowder civ. Janissaries, Hand Cannons, free gold-miner upgrades and faster gunpowder.
Turks.watch_for: Early Hand Cannon/Janissary timings; note they have no Halberdier or Elite Skirmisher.

Vietnamese.playstyle: Archer civ. Rattan Archers with high pierce armor, Imperial Skirmishers, and they start seeing the enemy.
Vietnamese.watch_for: Rattan Archers beat other archers in the pierce-armor war.

Vikings.playstyle: Infantry and naval civ. Cheap +HP infantry (Berserks), free Wheelbarrow/Hand Cart eco, and Longboats.
Vikings.watch_for: Strong economy, Berserk infantry, and a dominant navy on water.
